pr.title: lb: name THP header fields and float constants in lbmthp
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Readability/naming follow-up for lbmthp: renamed THPDecComp header fields from unk_08..unk_28 to THP header names, added named static constants for sdata2 float/f64 values, and slightly improved fn_8001EBF0 fuzzy match by folding ALIGN_32 into the assignment site. No new 100% function matches were claimed.

key_files.role: Updated call-site field references to the new THPDecComp names, changed fn_8001EBF0 ALIGN_32 assignment shape, and replaced inline float literals with named constants.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbmthp.c hunks for fn_8001EB14, fn_8001EBF0, fn_8001ECF4, lbMthp_8001F410, lbMthp_8001F67C

key_files.role: Renamed THPDecComp fields at offsets 0x08..0x28 from unk_* placeholders to THP header field names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: THPDecComp version, buf_size, x_size, y_size, frame_rate, num_frames, first_frame, frame_offsets, first_frame_size

key_files.role: Replaced unused placeholder static floats with named static const float/f64 constants at 804D7CC8, 804D7CD0, and 804D7CD8.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: static const lbl_804D7CC8, lbl_804D7CD0, lbl_804D7CD8
pr_body: named .sdata2 float constants and f64 constant

what_changed: THPDecComp offsets 0x08..0x28 were renamed from unk_08, unk_0C, unk_10, unk_14, unk_18, unk_1C, unk_20, unk_24, unk_28 to version, buf_size, x_size, y_size, frame_rate, num_frames, first_frame, frame_offsets, first_frame_size.
Uses in fn_8001EB14 and fn_8001ECF4 were updated to the renamed THPDecComp fields, including OSReport diagnostics for the THP header values.
fn_8001EBF0 changed local declaration order and folded ALIGN_32(data->unk_100) into the aligned_100 assignment instead of assigning data->unk_100 first and aligning later.
lbMthp_8001F410 changed the 0.016666668f multiplier to lbl_804D7CC8.
lbMthp_8001F67C changed repeated GXInitTexObjLOD 0.0f arguments to lbl_804D7CD8.
lbmthp.static.h removed placeholder static floats lb_804D7CC0 and lbl_804D7CE0 and added const lbl_804D7CC8, lbl_804D7CD0, and lbl_804D7CD8.
PR body claimed python configure.py && ninja was green, mn/gm consumers of lbmthp.h built cleanly, and previous lbmthp.c matched-function status was preserved.
Bot report recorded one unmatched-item improvement for main/melee/lb/lbmthp fn_8001EBF0 from 98.77% to 99.23%; the PR body states no new 100% matches.

smart_moves.text: Renamed struct fields in the owning header and then updated consumers, rather than adding source-local aliases or continuing unk_* field references.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbmthp.c use-site updates in fn_8001EB14 and fn_8001ECF4

smart_moves.text: Kept the matching claim narrow: only a fuzzy improvement for fn_8001EBF0 was claimed, with no new 100% match claim.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report for fn_8001EBF0 98.77% to 99.23%

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a binary/header format layout is understood, replacing contiguous unk_* struct fields with semantic field names in the owning header improves reviewability and lets call sites document the data flow.
evidence_refs: pr_body: THPDecComp field names replacing unk_08..unk_28
raw_diff: THPDecComp and use-site field replacements

decomp_lessons.lesson: For matching cleanups, a small source-shape change such as combining ALIGN_32 with the assignment site can improve a fuzzy score without claiming a full match.

Data-section naming work may be lower priority than completing text/code matching for the whole translation unit; reviewer feedback explicitly discouraged spending effort on data-section matching before the TU code is matched.
evidence_refs: review by ribbanya: waste of time to try to get data section to match before code for entire TU is matched
issue_comment by itsgrimetime: acknowledged workflow opened/committed PR before catching it

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Fold ALIGN_32 into the first assignment to aligned_100 and adjust local declaration order in fn_8001EBF0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.result: Bot and PR body report fuzzy improvement from 98.77% to 99.23%, not a full match.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8001EBF0 local order and aligned_100 assignment

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduce static const float/f64 symbols for data-section constants used in lbmthp.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.result: Merged, but reviewer feedback cautioned that data-section matching before the whole TU code is matched is generally not worth the effort.
evidence_refs: review by ribbanya: data section matching before TU code matched

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: It is a waste of time to try to get the data section to match before the code for the entire TU is matched; fine to merge, but generally not worth bothering.

review_feedback.author: itsgrimetime
review_feedback.feedback: Author acknowledged the reviewer point and said the PR was opened/committed by a new workflow before they caught it.

lbmthp static const lbl_804D7CC8 lbl_804D7CD0 lbl_804D7CD8
review data section match before code entire TU matched

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, state, timestamps, changed files
human_pr_text: PR body and issue comment excerpt
raw_diff: complete diff for 3 changed files
text_corpus.jsonl: PR body, bot report, review, author comment
notes: The PR body claims local build and consumer verification, but no raw build log is included in the slice.
No inline review comments are present; review feedback is from one approval review body plus author acknowledgement.
The PR evidence supports the field names and constants as changed in this PR, but the slice does not independently prove the THP file-format semantics beyond the author's description and code usage.
The reviewer allowed merge while discouraging the data-section focus; this should not be read as a rejection of the specific merged constants.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer text/TU code matching before data-section polish
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2506 was merged, but the approval review cautioned that trying to match the data section before the entire translation unit's code is matched is generally not worth the effort. Use this as historical support for text-before-data prioritization, especially when a PR is otherwise a readability/naming follow-up with no new 100% matches.
evidence_refs: pr_body: no new 100% matches; readability/naming follow-up

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header-owned struct field naming can clarify binary format use-sites
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2506 renamed THPDecComp header fields in lbmthp.h and updated lbmthp.c use sites, replacing raw unk_* fields with version, buffer size, dimensions, frame rate/count, and frame offset/size names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8001EB14 and fn_8001ECF4 use-site updates

source_update_candidates.title: Add PR #2506 as supporting example for text-before-data matching
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2506 is a concrete historical example where a reviewer approved but cautioned against spending time on data-section matching before the whole TU code is matched. It may be useful as an example under the existing text-before-data-matching standard rather than as a new rule.
source_update_candidates.reason: The current standard already covers the rule; curator may want to attach this PR as historical evidence/example.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the named float/f64 constants as a general style rule; the same PR contains reviewer feedback discouraging data-section matching before the TU code is matched.
Do not claim any new 100% function match from this PR; the PR body explicitly says there were no new 100% matches.
Do not infer that the reviewer objected to merging the PR; the review says it was fine to merge and was approved.
Do not treat the PR body's build verification as independently reproduced by this intake; no build was run and no full raw build log is loaded.
